demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged care facility; requires assistance with daily activities because of poor eyesight

contact_nok.relationship: aged care facility nursing staff (present and made the call)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Nursing staff calling on behalf of a resident: 'She's very upset this evening — she keeps seeing things that aren't there and it's distressing her.' The resident herself says: 'I know they aren't real, but there are miniature people in brightly coloured clothes marching across my bed, and it is terribly upsetting.'
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last 12 hours she has suddenly started seeing vivid, detailed images that come and go, it is worse tonight in the dim quiet room, and she is distressed and not sleeping; staff want advice on what to do.

symptom_narrative.character: vivid, detailed pictures that appear and disappear — miniature people in colourful clothes, faces, animals and geometric patterns
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she finds it terribly upsetting even though she says she knows the images are not real
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: she sees them in the room, especially on and around the bed
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse this evening when the room is dim and quiet, better with light and activity per staff
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: staff notice they ease when the lights are on or there is more going on around her
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: low light, being alone and quiet in the room in the evening
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: she says she does not hear any voices, does not feel anything touching her, and does not smell anything unusual — only the images
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: staff are not aware of this happening before; it started today
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: distressed and not sleeping; agitated but still cooperative and able to be redirected

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing severe loss of vision in both eyes from an eye condition affecting the central vision (age-related macular degeneration)
past_medical_history.how_managed: under eye specialist care; needs help with daily activities because of poor sight

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: regular facility medications; staff report no new medicines were started recently

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged care facility; the room is dimly lit, quiet and with little going on around her; nursing staff present and involved in her care

objective_data_offered.value: blood pressure 142/85, heart rate 82, temperature 36.7 degrees, breathing rate 16, oxygen saturation 98%
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: read out by facility nursing staff from routine ward observations, not clinician-verified

objective_data_offered.value: she is awake and alert, watching and pointing at things on the bed that are not there, but holds a clear and logical conversation, is easily redirected, and keeps saying she understands the images are not real
staff report she recognises the nurses, knows where she is, and is oriented — she is upset by the images rather than confused about her surroundings
her room is dimly lit, quiet and lacks stimulation; the hallucinations seem to flare in these low-light, low-stimulation conditions
